This message was automatically generated.
Failed unit tests are unrelated. The both passed in my local environment.

Thanks for updating ! looks good overall, last comment:
- CONTAINER_RESOURCE_CHANGED -  it's possible that containerImpl may receive this event other than RUNNING state, we may need to handle and ignore this event at other states too. Otherwise, the container state-machine will receive InvalidEventException. Do we then need to handle this event at every state after RUNNING ? Or Given that current containerImpl resize logic is fairly light weighted, we can update the container.resource synchronously from ContainerMonitorImpl. we may change this to be asynchronous if needed in the future if the update logic becomes heavy. thoughts?

Had an offline discussion with Wangda,  given that the current update logic is fairly light weighted and even the nm-state-store operation happens synchronously at user-level API, we may also synchronously update the container size on the increaseContainersResource call ? The benefit is that user can get fast notification that the container is re-sized immediately when the call returns. We can always re-visit this part if this really becomes a bottleneck as the API itself won't change either ways.
This sounds like a good idea to me. Let me play with it and report back.

Attaching patch that updates container resource synchronously. In particular:
* Call ContainersMonitor event handler directly in ContainerManagerImpl
* Remove {{ChangeContainerResourceEvent}}, {{ContainerResourceChangedEvent}}, and related logic from {{ContainerImpl}}
* Add {{setResource}} function to {{ContainerImpl}} (called by {{ContainersMonitorImpl}} in YARN-1643) to update container resource.

Failed unit tests are unrelated. The both passed in my local environment.

Thanks for updating ! looks good overall, last comment:
- CONTAINER_RESOURCE_CHANGED -  it's possible that containerImpl may receive this event other than RUNNING state, we may need to handle and ignore this event at other states too. Otherwise, the container state-machine will receive InvalidEventException. Do we then need to handle this event at every state after RUNNING ? Or Given that current containerImpl resize logic is fairly light weighted, we can update the container.resource synchronously from ContainerMonitorImpl. we may change this to be asynchronous if needed in the future if the update logic becomes heavy. thoughts?

Had an offline discussion with Wangda,  given that the current update logic is fairly light weighted and even the nm-state-store operation happens synchronously at user-level API, we may also synchronously update the container size on the increaseContainersResource call ? The benefit is that user can get fast notification that the container is re-sized immediately when the call returns. We can always re-visit this part if this really becomes a bottleneck as the API itself won't change either ways.
This sounds like a good idea to me. Let me play with it and report back.

Attaching patch that updates container resource synchronously. In particular:
* Call ContainersMonitor event handler directly in ContainerManagerImpl
* Remove {{ChangeContainerResourceEvent}}, {{ContainerResourceChangedEvent}}, and related logic from {{ContainerImpl}}
* Add {{setResource}} function to {{ContainerImpl}} (called by {{ContainersMonitorImpl}} in YARN-1643) to update container resource.
